creator: peter.jeremy@alcatel.com.au
text:
On 2003-Mar-25 12:44:48 -0700, Marc Rochkind <rochkind@basepath.com> wrote:
>As I understand the bug system, this one is OPEN and NON-CRITICAL.

Yes.  No-one feels it is important enough to apply.

>Actually, I would view issue #5 as listed as EXTREMELY CRITICAL. Not 
>setting otime when a semop is performed means that there's no way to get a 
>semaphore initialized with portable code. (Typically, the creator of the 
>semaphore initializes it and then does a semop to set the time. All other 
>users wait for the time to become non-zero.)

I don't follow this.  There's no provision in the semaphore handling
functions to wait for a non-zero otime - a process would need to poll
otime using semctl(..., IPC_STAT, ...).

All the semaphore code I've seen can be split into two categories:
Critical region serialisation or producer/consumer.  In the first
case, when a process finds the semaphore doesn't exist, it creates it
and then initialises it to 1 if it was successful - once the semaphore
exists, processes just do a wait (pass -1 as sem_op).  In the second
case, any process can create the semphore and the producer process
will increment it as appropriate.

Can you please explain a situation where otime is critical.

Peter
